author: Coffee Nodes
msg:
1 - what's true today might not be true tomorrow (when 28 out of 30 tasks are not out yet). 
2 - if they do it, everybody will replicate, and suddenly it looses value because the unity network benefits from diversity in setup, devices, connections etc. The more people have the same setup, the more rewards will go to the ones who have a unique one, as their data will somehow be more valuable

author: Matt
msg: Once a payment is made there is potential for  loss in a contract. The credit's duration is a separate issue.

msg:
Yeah timer plug the way to go, have mine set to twice daily 45mins each.. also worth setting max charge on each phone to 85% if you want the battery to last longer
Not all phones have this option but I think most androids do

Also I turn off fast charge, again for battery preservation

msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - Global Footprint**

The Unity Network spans the globe.

**Current reach:
**110+ countries
1,100+ cities
Tens of thousands of unique IP addresses
Diverse device manufacturers and OS versions

Every new device strengthens coverage. Every new region expands potential. Every new operator contributes to the world's largest distributed telecommunications verification network.

Multiple task families are being deployed to the network, and in future releases, developers will be invited to build on Unity Network.

Geographic diversity is a competitive advantage. The network grows stronger with every location added.

Learn more about Unity Network at unitynodes.io.

ULOs that found this APP name somewhere and downloaded it can't see any info on licenses, uptime and  mostly.. How the f..k can they know what UPs is😅

author: ⚡️BeyondLimitsNodes⚡️
msg:
Imagine, you've read about Unity APP on X. Downloaded it, and then got a license. You don't know what UNO/ULO is, what's the uptime, What's UPs, when first rewards and why they are even paid for. 
Then the second day you see your first 0.04UPs. And start looking for withdrawals. Dead end. Can't find it. You think must be another 'scam'.. You delete the APP.. That's how it goes for now with people that don't have our support

author: VeganCatLover
msg: I can see that the the phone isn't connected to wifi. I've had an issue a few times where out of a farm of 10 phones, 8 of them just disconnect from the wifi and I need to restart the router for them to reconnect. Bloody annoying as aside from that they usually behave themselves.

author: Krabalot
msg:
@Lorizzle1 @mseidelin I’m thinking about this small setup to try for ~20 WiFi 5 phones: ISP router (modem mode) -> UCG-Ultra (cloud gateway)-> USW-Lite-8-PoE (switch) -> U6-Pro (AP). 

Any advice/critiques?

author: Lorizzle
msg: Seems like it would be pretty straightforward. I might go to a higher model of the gateway if you plan on getting faster than 1Gig in the near future?

author: Krabalot
msg: Yeah. I want to keep it as minimal as possible for now, but you’re right

author: MSA | Valhalla.Node
msg: Shouldn’t be any issues with the hardware. I’d suggest you try a day or two just with one or two phones, just to establish a baseline reward level before scaling up to 20 and beyond

author: Mat
msg: Question: with S&R we will need a separate SIM I believe, why is that? Can I not use my normal sim and just add international calling?

author: Lorizzle
msg: Yeah might not be feasible, as the other products you mentioned are also limited to 1Gig and they would have to be upgraded too
